heading: Licensing or certification of locomotive operators
label: 20135 Licensing or certification of locomotive operators

children.heading: .—General
children.label: a .—General
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation shall prescribe regulations and issue orders to establish a program requiring the licensing or certification, after one year after the program is established, of any operator of a locomotive.
shall be carried out through review and approval of each railroad carrier’s operator qualification standards;
shall require comprehensive knowledge of applicable railroad carrier operating practices and rules;

children.label: A
children.text: any denial, cancellation, revocation, or suspension of a motor vehicle operator’s license by a State for cause within the prior 5 years; and

children.label: B
children.text: any conviction within the prior 5 years of an offense described in section 30304(a)(3)(A) or (B) of this title;
may require, based on the individual’s driving record, disqualification or the granting of a license or certification conditioned on requirements the Secretary prescribes; and

children.label: A
children.text: to request the chief driver licensing official of each State in which the individual has held a motor vehicle operator’s license within the prior 5 years to provide information about the individual’s driving record to the individual’s employer, prospective employer, or the Secretary, as the Secretary requires; and

children.label: B
children.text: to make the request provided for in for information to be sent to the individual’s employer, prospective employer, or the Secretary, as the Secretary requires.section 30305(b)(4) of this title

The Secretary shall prescribe standards and establish procedures for waiving subsection (b)(4) of this section for an individual or class of individuals who the Secretary decides are not currently unfit to operate a locomotive. However, the Secretary may waive subsection (b)(4) for an individual or class of individuals with a conviction, cancellation, revocation, or suspension described in paragraph (2)(A) or (B) of this subsection only if the individual or class, after the conviction, cancellation, revocation, or suspension, successfully completes a rehabilitation program established by a railroad carrier or approved by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: a conviction for operating a motor vehicle when under the influence of, or impaired by, alcohol or a controlled substance; or

children.label: B
children.text: the cancellation, revocation, or suspension of the individual’s motor vehicle operator’s license for operating a motor vehicle when under the influence of, or impaired by, alcohol or a controlled substance.

children.heading: .—Opportunity for Hearing
children.label: d .—Opportunity for Hearing
children.text: An individual denied a license or certification or whose license or certification is conditioned on requirements prescribed under subsection (b)(4) of this section shall be entitled to a hearing under to decide whether the license has been properly denied or conditioned.section 20103(e) of this title

children.heading: .—Opportunity to Examine and Comment on Information
children.label: e .—Opportunity to Examine and Comment on Information
children.text: The Secretary, employer, or prospective employer, as appropriate, shall make information obtained under subsection (b)(6) of this section available to the individual. The individual shall be given an opportunity to comment in writing about the information. Any comment shall be included in any record or file maintained by the Secretary, employer, or prospective employer that contains information to which the comment is related.
